input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:06:16.712Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The `Light` value is used when the OS is in light mode, the `Dark` value when in dark mode. Changes apply **automatically** at runtime — no event handling needed.

---

**C# equivalent — use `SetAppTheme<T>()`:**

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

`SetAppTheme<T>()` takes the bindable property and two values (light, dark). It reacts to system theme changes the same way the XAML version does.

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Responds to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **No boilerplate** | No need to subscribe to `RequestedThemeChanged` — bindings update automatically |
| **Scope** | Works on any bindable property (colors, images, strings, etc.) |
| **Override** | Set `Application.UserAppTheme` in code to force a specific theme, ignoring the OS setting |
